title: Field activity changed; the LEV proof gap needs checking
summary: No May result changed the LEV outlook. The useful trial read was mostly absence of results: several watched studies were complete or near expected completion with no posted outcomes in available public records, while the field still lacked repeated human evidence that aging-relevant health or function improves for longer. The important question is whether the evidence makes LEV look closer, or only clarifies where evidence remains thin.
current_evidence_picture: The overall field read is human biomarker signal, with mixed momentum and a provisional read. The main evidence gap remains: The field still lacks repeated human studies showing long-lasting improvements in health or function in ways that clearly add up toward LEV.
what_changed: Recent field updates include: Extracellular Vesicle and Secretome Therapies Surveillance for June 15, 2026. Their importance depends on whether they change human outcomes, safety, read firmness, or the main evidence gap; more activity by itself is not progress toward LEV.

before_now_next.label: Before
before_now_next.title: Isolated claims
before_now_next.summary: A few prominent intervention stories could make the field look more advanced than it was. Many claims were hard to compare because they used different endpoints, populations, and standards of proof.

before_now_next.label: Now
before_now_next.title: Early signals, uneven strength
before_now_next.summary: More areas now have human biomarkers, narrow functional signs, disease outcomes, or trials in motion. Most still stop short of showing broad, lasting human aging benefit.

before_now_next.label: Next
before_now_next.title: Human proof that lasts
before_now_next.summary: The next major step is repeated human evidence that older adults maintain better function for longer, not just short-term biomarker movement or narrow disease response.

recent_developments.label: Extracellular Vesicle and Secretome Therapies Surveillance for June 15, 2026
recent_developments.summary: Staged surveillance update adding the June 2026 Frontiers in Aging APRC-CM multimodal healthy-aging pilot, refreshing stale registry metadata for four published EV/secretome human study records, and keeping the public stage, momentum, and confidence unchanged.
recent_developments.impact_on_outlook: This may affect the overall field read only if it changes human outcomes, safety, read firmness, or the main evidence gap.

what_to_watch_next.label: Human studies with long-lasting functional improvement
what_to_watch_next.summary: This is the missing proof that would move the story from interesting research to meaningful LEV progress.
what_to_watch_next.what_to_look_for: Trials that show long-lasting functional improvement in aging-relevant populations, not only biomarker movement or narrow disease response.

what_to_watch_next.label: Fast-moving but still uncertain tracks
what_to_watch_next.summary: Partial reprogramming now has a visible ER-100 phase 1 registry, while immune clearance of senescent cells, extracellular vesicle approaches, and microbiome restoration also have visible motion but still need stronger links to useful human results and safety.
what_to_watch_next.what_to_look_for: Posted human data, long-lasting effects, delivery control, and clear separation between biological excitement and clinical usefulness.

what_to_watch_next.label: Updates that actually change the outlook
what_to_watch_next.summary: Some near-term progress may come from clearer evidence rather than new results. That still matters when it shows which claims are solid, thin, or overclaimed.
what_to_watch_next.what_to_look_for: Evidence strong enough to change a rating, the firmness of the read, or the main evidence gap for a hallmark or track.

where_better_evidence_is_needed.label: Separate aging evidence from nearby signals
where_better_evidence_is_needed.rationale: The field has enough activity to look busy and enough weak evidence to invite overstatement. The next useful work is separating direct aging evidence from nearby disease or biomarker evidence.
where_better_evidence_is_needed.what_better_evidence_would_show: The most valuable updates will clarify whether early human signals are about aging itself, nearby disease, or short-term biomarkers.

where_better_evidence_is_needed.label: Trial starts are not proof
where_better_evidence_is_needed.rationale: Several tracks now have active or recruiting human studies. The value comes when those studies produce clear results, safety boundaries, or clear nulls.
where_better_evidence_is_needed.what_better_evidence_would_show: The important signal is whether those studies report useful outcomes, acceptable safety, or clear null results.

where_better_evidence_is_needed.label: Close the gap between biology and benefit
where_better_evidence_is_needed.rationale: Some hallmarks have many biology ideas but fewer clean intervention programs that can be tested against aging-relevant human outcomes.
where_better_evidence_is_needed.what_better_evidence_would_show: Areas with thin evidence need clearer human endpoints and milestone criteria before they can carry much weight.

what_would_change_the_outlook.label: Repeated human functional results
what_would_change_the_outlook.summary: Several independent studies show that older adults maintain better function for longer, not just that biomarkers move.

what_would_change_the_outlook.label: Safer delivery and lasting effects
what_would_change_the_outlook.summary: Fast-moving tracks show controlled delivery, acceptable safety, and benefits that last beyond short-term signals.

what_would_change_the_outlook.label: Null, unsafe, or narrow human results
what_would_change_the_outlook.summary: Human studies fail to show useful outcomes, reveal safety problems, or show effects that are too narrow to change the aging story.

track_examples_to_inspect.label: Promising but uncertain
track_examples_to_inspect.title: Microbiome metabolite restoration
track_examples_to_inspect.summary: There are human functional signals, but the evidence is still narrow and not yet proof of broad aging benefit.

track_examples_to_inspect.label: Fast-moving frontier
track_examples_to_inspect.title: Partial reprogramming
track_examples_to_inspect.summary: The animal source map is stronger and ER-100 is now a no-results human registry, but human outcomes, delivery control, and long-lasting safety remain the key questions.

track_examples_to_inspect.label: Clear bottleneck
track_examples_to_inspect.title: Stem-cell replacement
track_examples_to_inspect.summary: Some human functional signal exists, but the field still has to separate general aging benefit from narrow repair stories.

revision.review_reason: Generated draft after current LEV story staleness check: 1 outlook-changing public update(s) are newer than the current story.

triggers.description: Review when a public update after the last story review affects any public outlook.
triggers.threshold: Any newer public update record with affected_outlook_ids.

triggers.description: Review when an overall, hallmark, or track evidence stage, field momentum, read firmness, main evidence gap, or strongest evidence changes.
Review immediately if the overall LEV timing-window interpretation changes.
Review when a new state-of-the-field edition is added or materially revised.
Review on the scheduled due date even if no material public event has occurred.
Review whenever the curator asks whether the public story still matches the evidence map.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The field still lacks repeated human studies showing long-lasting improvements in health or function in ways that clearly add up toward LEV.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: The recent picture is broader rather than stronger: updates in senomorphics, NAD/redox, stem-cell rejuvenation, mitophagy, chaperone support, autophagy induction, senolytics, and partial reprogramming are making the evidence easier to judge without making LEV look closer.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Metformin is clinically familiar and biologically broad, but direct human geroscience evidence is still mostly biomarker, transcriptomic, and short-term evidence that the intervention changed its intended biological target rather than long-lasting function or multimorbidity outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: MILES provides the main direct human biomarker anchor: short-term metformin changed glucose handling and skeletal-muscle and adipose transcriptomic pathways in older adults with impaired glucose tolerance.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The best-controlled 12-month spermidine trial in older adults was null on its primary memory endpoint and secondary outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: A 2026 spermidine pilot in older vaccine non-responders linked autophagy-related B-cell signals with improved vaccine-response markers, while remaining small and exploratory.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The strongest human functional and durability signal is concentrated in Niemann-Pick disease type C, a rare neurovisceral disease, not broad aging or LEV-relevant proteostasis restoration.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Arimoclomol slowed NPC disease progression in a randomized phase 2/3 trial, an open-label extension reported longer-term NPC disease and safety context, and FDA approved Miplyffa with miglustat for NPC neurological symptoms.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The strongest human outcomes are cardiovascular secondary-prevention results in high-risk disease populations, not broad healthy-aging or LEV-relevant rejuvenation outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: CANTOS showed that IL-1beta inhibition can reduce recurrent cardiovascular events without lipid lowering in post-MI patients with residual inflammatory risk.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The most direct aging and healthspan evidence remains animal evidence from MSC-derived EV and conditioned-medium studies.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Human split-face and meta-analytic dermatology evidence suggests stem-cell conditioned media or exosome-containing solutions can move skin-aging appearance and instrumented biomarker endpoints.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: No direct treated-human aging trial was identified for engineered or checkpoint-based immune clearance of senescent cells.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Anti-uPAR CAR T cells cleared senescent cells and restored tissue homeostasis in mouse pathology models, then improved exercise capacity, metabolic function, and intestinal regeneration phenotypes in aged mice.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The human functional rating is narrow and rests mainly on urolithin A muscle endurance or strength signals, not replicated long-lasting frailty, mobility, disease, or mortality outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Urolithin A has randomized human trials with muscle endurance or strength signals, mitochondrial or inflammatory biomarker movement, and a 2024 systematic review summarizing both the signal and the lack of broader physical-function benefit.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The highest-quality human signal is structured exercise, not a druglike mitochondrial-biogenesis or dynamics therapy.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Supervised HIIT and combined training improved older-adult aerobic capacity, while HIIT increased skeletal-muscle mitochondrial respiration, mitochondrial protein synthesis, and mitochondrial protein abundance.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Human evidence is concentrated in short urolithin A studies with mitochondrial, muscle, immune, or recovery biomarkers; long-lasting clinical or healthy-lifespan benefit has not been shown.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Urolithin A has human target-engagement and biomarker support, including first-in-human mitochondrial signatures, ENERGIZE older-adult muscle-endurance and biomarker signals, ATLAS middle-aged strength and mitochondrial biomarkers, and MitoImmune immune-cell metabolism or function movement.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Human evidence is heterogeneous: NR, NMN, MIB-626, GlyNAC, MitoQ, nicotinamide-adjacent records, exercise co-interventions, and disease-context NAD uses should not be collapsed into one efficacy claim.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: GlyNAC produced the strongest older-adult functional signal, improving redox, mitochondrial, inflammatory, endothelial, physical-function, strength, and exercise-capacity measures in a small 16-week RCT.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The strongest human intervention signal is TRIIM, a nine-participant uncontrolled male pilot with epigenetic-age and immunosenescence biomarkers rather than randomized functional, disease, or mortality endpoints.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: TRIIM reported lower DNA-methylation epigenetic age and immunosenescence-marker movement after one year in nine healthy men.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Human outcomes evidence is still absent: a recruiting ER-100 phase 1 ocular safety registry is now visible, but there are no posted human safety, target-engagement, functional, disease, or aging results.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Cyclic OSKM partial reprogramming improved age-associated hallmarks, prolonged lifespan in a premature-aging mouse model, and improved recovery from metabolic disease and muscle injury in older wild-type mice.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Human biomarker evidence is direct but preliminary: the DKD D+Q pilot reported senescent-cell and SASP reductions, while the larger postmenopausal bone RCT had a null primary endpoint with exploratory subgroup signals.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: D+Q has the clearest direct human senolytic biomarker signal: a nine-person diabetic kidney disease pilot reported reduced adipose and skin senescence markers plus circulating SASP factors after short-course treatment.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Direct human evidence is small, local, or acute-stress focused: topical rapamycin skin p16 and metformin disuse-recovery muscle SASP markers are biomarker signals, not long-lasting aging outcomes.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Topical rapamycin reduced p16INK4A and increased collagen VII in a small human skin-aging trial.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: Direct human evidence is ex vivo, biopsy-biomarker evidence, or no-results registry context, not a tested in vivo stem-cell rejuvenation therapy.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: Older human HSCs show Cdc42-linked aging phenotypes, and ex vivo CASIN can repolarize aged human HSCs while shifting xenotransplant behavior toward young-donor patterns.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The strongest direct aging-relevant signal is laromestrocel in frailty, but allogeneic MSC benefit may reflect paracrine, immunovascular, or tissue-support effects rather than long-lasting stem-cell replacement.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: A 2026 randomized phase 2b frailty trial reported clinically meaningful 6-minute walk improvement after allogeneic MSC therapy, with a possible TIE2 biomarker response.

observed_outlook_states.evidence_gap: The direct transposon-control intervention evidence is still mostly animal, preprint, open-label human biomarker, or no-results trial records to watch; no controlled human aging-function trial has shown benefit from reverse transcriptase inhibition.
observed_outlook_states.strongest_evidence: SIRT6 and senescence studies give coherent LINE1 suppression biology, including evidence that SIRT6 repression fails with stress or age and that LINE1 cDNA can drive type I interferon activation.
